behaviors.description: Log entries with the same trace ID are grouped together regardless of which microservice emitted them.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the stated failure is grouping logic correctness on input records, not a required module-handoff failure. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no OS/network/subprocess requirement appears in the behavior text. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher level: behavior text only asserts grouping by `trace ID` across records; it does not require runtime infrastructure or cross-process contracts.
behaviors.test_description: Test the pure grouping function with mixed-service log fixtures sharing trace IDs; assert one group per trace ID and that service name differences do not split groups.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: A unit test directly exercises the exact failure boundary (wrong grouping key/use of service as accidental discriminator).

behaviors.description: The timeline view orders entries by timestamp, correctly handling clock skew of up to 500ms between services.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is about timeline output semantics that commonly depend on interaction between correlation metadata and timeline rendering/ordering modules. Unit may miss handoff bugs where skew-adjusted timestamps are not propagated. REAL-INFRA: no explicit real OS/network/subprocess dependency in behavior text, so not System. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System: behavior text specifies ordering logic and skew tolerance, not real infrastructure behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire ingest/correlation + timeline-ordering modules in-process, feed multi-service traces with controlled ±500ms skew, and assert final rendered sequence matches expected causal/timestamp order.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The described test requires module wiring and state handoff, matching Integration as minimum.

behaviors.description: A latency spike (p99 above 3x the baseline) is detected and flagged in the anomaly report.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure can occur at boundary between metric computation (baseline/p99) and anomaly-report emission; unit-only p99 math can pass while report flagging fails. REAL-INFRA: behavior text does not require real network/OS/subprocess. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System/Agentic: no real infra condition and no model-output dependence are stated.
behaviors.test_description: Run metrics aggregation and anomaly-report generation together on synthetic latency distributions where p99 crosses and does not cross 3x baseline; assert flag presence/absence in produced report.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Covers cross-module handoff from detector to report, which is the real failure boundary in the behavior wording.

behaviors.description: Malformed JSON log entries are skipped without crashing the pipeline, and the count of skipped entries is reported.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior explicitly spans parser error handling, pipeline continuity, and reporting of skipped count, which is a multi-module state-propagation boundary. REAL-INFRA: no explicit requirement for real filesystem/network/subprocess in text. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System: behavior says malformed entries and pipeline/reporting semantics, not OS/event/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute parse -> ingest pipeline -> summary reporting in-process with mixed valid/malformed JSON; assert processing continues, valid entries are preserved, and skipped-count metric equals malformed input count.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is needed because the failure can hide in parser-to-pipeline-to-report handoff, beyond a single function.

behaviors.description: The tool handles a burst of 10,000 log entries per second without dropping entries or falling behind.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this is an explicit throughput/backpressure runtime condition (`10,000 log entries per second`) where scheduler, buffering, and runtime I/O behavior can cause drops/lag; in-process fakes can hide these failures (MOCK-HIDE). MOCK-MISS: interaction with real execution environment is part of the failure boundary. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Integration: behavior text names a real load/performance condition, which is disqualified by purely in-process wiring tests.
behaviors.test_description: Run the actual tool process under a realistic burst generator at 10k entries/s, measure input vs processed counts and processing lag over time, and assert zero drops plus bounded backlog/latency growth.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test must exercise real runtime behavior under load; lower-level tests cannot reliably expose drop/behind failures.
